title: pilosella fusca subsp. chrysanthes
intro: Pilosella fusca subsp. chrysanthes is a plant species commonly known as a type of yellow hawkweed, which is a flowering plant in the dandelion family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone compounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Pilosella fusca subsp. chrysanthes can be used in herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, to support overall health and well-being.
